by the High Court. Besides, Rule 6 A is also in violation of 
International Principles and Guidelines on Access to Justice 
for Persons with Disabilities 2019. Para 7.1 of these guidelines 
reads, “The right to equal access to justice requires that 
persons with disabilities have the opportunity to participate 
directly in adjudicative processes and be involved in various 
roles in the administration of justice on an equal basis with 
others. States should ensure that persons with disabilities 
are able to act as judges, lawyers, prosecutors, witnesses, 
250 
Supreme Court Reports
jurors, experts and court officials in the justice system without 
discrimination.” In this connection, para 7.2(b) also reads, 
“Remove all disability-related barriers, including laws, that 
prevent persons with disabilities from being judges or jurors 
or serving in any other justice related positions”. The learned 
counsel submitted that these guidelines are in direct response
